Title: Experienced Scientist – Human Data & Target Discovery
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Gubra, we are expanding our Drug Discovery Innovation capabilities with a strengthened focus on integrating human data, computational approaches, and disease biology into early discovery.




We believe the next generation of therapeutic breakthroughs will come from combining deep biological understanding with large-scale human datasets, translational insight, and innovative target discovery approaches.




This role offers a unique opportunity to help shape Gubra’s next-generation discovery engine by strengthening how we identify, prioritize, and advance novel therapeutic opportunities across cardiometabolic disease, inflammation & immunology, and age-related diseases.




You will have significant scientific influence and contribute both to discovery programs and to the development of scalable and innovative approaches across early discovery.







Your new department

Drug Discovery Innovation (DDI) is at the forefront of Gubra’s efforts to build the next generation of early therapeutic discovery.




The department combines expertise across disease biology, translational science, computational discovery, and early therapeutic research to identify and advance novel therapeutic opportunities within cardiometabolic disease, inflammation & immunology, and age-related diseases.




As part of Gubra’s strengthened focus on pipeline innovation and early discovery, DDI plays a central role in shaping discovery strategies, driving target identification efforts, and building scalable, data-driven discovery capabilities.




We work in a highly collaborative and interdisciplinary environment where scientific curiosity, translational thinking, and innovation are central to how we discover and advance new therapeutic opportunities.







The role

As an experienced Scientist, you will work at the interface of biology, human data, and early drug discovery to identify and prioritize novel therapeutic targets.




You will play a central role in translating large-scale human datasets, omics analyses, and computational insights into actionable biological hypotheses and discovery opportunities.




Working closely with cross-functional teams, you will help strengthen Gubra’s data-driven discovery capabilities and contribute to innovative approaches for target identification and evaluation.




The level of the position will be matched to the qualifications and experience of the candidate.







Your responsibilities

Drive discovery campaigns focused on identifying and advancing novel therapeutic targets
Leverage human genetics, omics datasets, and public databases to strengthen target prioritization and mechanistic understanding
Integrate disease biology, translational insights, and competitive intelligence to define target hypotheses and assess opportunities and risks
Collaborate across scientific disciplines to support early validation and progression of target concepts
Deliver clear scientific recommendations to support data-driven decision-making
Contribute to the development of scalable computational and data-driven approaches for target discovery and prioritization
Establish and maintain productive external scientific collaborations where relevant







Your profile

You hold a PhD in life sciences and bring experience from biotech and/or pharmaceutical drug discovery environments.




In addition, you will likely:

Have 5+ years of experience within target identification and early drug discovery
Have hands-on experience applying human data, omics, and computational approaches in therapeutic discovery
Bring strong disease biology expertise within cardiometabolic disease, inflammation & immunology, and/or age-related diseases
Be able to independently drive scientific initiatives and target evaluations
Thrive in collaborative and interdisciplinary environments
Possess strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills




Experience translating complex human datasets into actionable biological insights and therapeutic hypotheses is considered an advantage.







Why join Gubra?

This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to building the next generation of data-driven drug discovery at Gubra.




You will join a highly collaborative organization where scientific innovation, agility, and translational thinking are central to how we discover and advance new therapeutic opportunities.




At Gubra, you will have the opportunity to combine biology and human data in an integrated discovery environment while helping shape modern approaches to early therapeutic discovery with direct impact on future pipeline development.







Contact and Application

Please apply using the ”Apply” button. The deadline is June 10, 2026. We will hold the first round of interviews during the week commencing June 15, 2026.




If you have questions regarding the role please contact Senior Director DDI, Maja Kirkegaard Jensen, mje@gubra.dk.




We are looking forward to receiving your application.







About Gubra

Gubra is an ambitious contract research organisation (CRO) and biotech company striving for excellence at all levels. We insist on doing things efficiently – and often differently - to reach the results we aim for. Our vision is to become leaders in the fight for a more sustainable and healthier world. We do that by facilitating the discovery of new medicine, and by acting and inspiring others to fight the ongoing climate and biodiversity crises.




Gubra’s activities are focused on the early stages of drug development and are organised in two highly synergistic business areas: CRO Services and Discovery & Partnerships (D&P). We generate our revenue by performing research for life science companies as well as by partnering projects from our discovery and development pipeline.




Our therapeutic focus is within metabolic and fibrotic diseases, and we specialize in in vivo pharmacology, ex vivo assays, drug profiling, histology, stereology and whole brain and organ imaging. In addition, we offer a full palette of advanced transcriptomics. Our ML/AI-driven peptide drug discovery platform streaMLine enables us to rapidly develop a peptide hit into a non-clinical candidate ready for development. Through a constant focus on high quality, scientific excellence, speed, and solid teamwork we have established ourselves as a highly professional and competent partner in the market.




People are our greatest asset, and our team consists of around 300 employees. The mix of people from different cultures and educational backgrounds combined with our entrepreneurial mindset have greatly impacted our working environment, which is characterized by entrepreneurial drive, scientific curiosity, and teamwork – we join forces!

Title: Open Talent Pool - all DHI locations
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At DHI, water, environment and sustainability meet innovation. Join us in enabling a sustainable future for water!

Looking to make an impact on our planet? At DHI, you can. We work with water in all its forms – combining deep domain knowledge, advanced digital technology, and more than 60 years of research and innovation to help society use, manage and live with water sustainably. With over 1,100 engineers and specialists across 26 countries, we are continually expanding our global community of experts.

As we grow, new opportunities emerge throughout the year. Even when a specific vacancy isn’t yet published, we welcome proactive applications from individuals who want to contribute to our mission – including senior specialist with years of experience or students, recent graduates and early career professionals who are excited to build their future in the water sector.

Why submit an open application?

Our business evolves quickly, and new roles – technical, scientific, digital and business-oriented – are created as projects grow and priorities shift. Submitting an open application adds you to our Open Talent Pool, enabling us to reach out when a suitable opportunity arises, often before it is advertised.

Before you submit your open application, please make sure to check our current vacancies as well.

Opportunities for Students & Early Career Talent

We strongly encourage students and early career professionals to apply.

At DHI, You Can


Join as an intern, student assistant, thesis collaboration partner or industry master program student
Contribute to global water projects alongside experienced specialists
Apply your academic knowledge to real-world environmental and digital challenges
Grow your skills in engineering, modelling, climate resilience, data science, software development, sustainability and more
Work in a culture that supports experimentation, learning and exploration


If you are studying or recently graduated in engineering, environmental science, climate studies, digital technology, or related fields – and want to help shape a sustainable future – your application is very welcome.

Profiles we are interested in

We invite applications from experienced professionals, graduates, and students with interest or expertise in:


Engineering & Environmental Science Coastal, marine, environmental, hydraulic, climate, civil, ecosystem modelling
Data, Digital & Technology Software development, data science, machine learning, AI, digital product development
Consulting & Project Delivery Advisory services, modelling, client engagement, project/programme management
Sustainability & ESG
Corporate Functions Legal, compliance, HR, finance, operations, commercial roles


We value cross disciplinary thinkers—if your background doesn’t fit into a category, we still encourage you to apply.

What We Offer

When you join DHI, you are empowered to be an explorer—to innovate, collaborate, and continuously learn. You’ll be part of a global organisation committed to protecting water ecosystems and developing sustainable solutions for communities, cities, industries and the planet.

Positions May Be


Fulltime or parttime
Based in various global locations or open to flexible arrangements
Project based or permanent, depending on organisational needs


How To Apply


Please submit your application through our careers page under “Open Talent”. If you are interested in other locations, please indicate that clearly.


Kindly Attach


Your CV
A brief note outlining your area of interests, skills and how you found DHI
List your preferred DHI location(s)
Links to portfolio, publications, or project work (optional)


Learn more about DHI www.dhigroup.com

Title: Research Engineer, Robotics Foundation Models
Description:
Research Engineer, Robotics Foundation Models at Qualia




Qualia is the company that brings foundation models into the real world: data collection, fine-tuning, inference, and on-device deployment, end to end. We're a small, technical team in Copenhagen and Stockholm building this for manufacturers, in partnership with top companies. Qualia has raised roughly USD 10M to pursue the hardest problems in robotics.




We're hiring a research engineer to push the model side of our research. VLAs, WAMs, that work on real customer data. RL that closes the gap on precision tasks. World models, UMI, EgoData, that let us iterate faster than collecting another 1,000 episodes of teleop.




This is not a research role at a lab. This is a research engineer role at a startup. Your work ships to customers, runs on robots, and gets judged by whether the task succeeds.




What you'll work on

VLA fine-tuning at the edge of what's possible
VLAs on customer and internal data. Make them work where the base model fails.
Build the recipe. Subtask decomposition, stage classifiers, relative actions, action chunking, normalization. Everything that turns "we got 30% success" into "we got 99% success."
Push the data pipeline upstream so that fine-tuning is repeatable across robots and tasks, not a one-off.




RL on top of VLAs

Residual policies for high-precision sub-movements (key insertion, peg-in-hole, force-sensitive contact).
RL-driven training loops on top of VLAs and WAMs.
Sim-to-real where it accelerates a real customer task, not for its own sake.




World models and synthetic data

Build the workflow that turns a small amount of real teleop into a much larger training corpus.
Decide what's worth using in our platform and what isn't.
You'll own model side end to end: data, training, inference, evaluation, iteration.




Who we're looking for:

Required

Strong research track record OR strong applied robotics ML track record. Either path works.
Hands-on with at least one VLA stack. Trained one, evaluated one, debugged one. Not just read the paper.
Comfortable with the main ML frameworks. Comfortable on Linux. Comfortable on a real robot (or eager to be).
Comfortable with messy data and the realization that 80% of the work is making the data not lie to you.
Comfortable shipping. Models in production beat models in arxiv at this company.




Bonus

Real teleoperation or data collection experience
RL beyond toy environments
Experience with sim-to-real, world models, or video-conditioned generation
Familiarity with VLAs
You've shipped a robot policy that a non-engineer used and didn't break




What we don't care about

Whether you have a PhD or you don't
Whether you've worked at a famous lab or you haven't
Whether your background is industry, academia, or self-taught




We care that you've done the thing.




How we work

Small team, fast loop. Around 10 people. Repos move fast. No PR purgatory.
Real customers from day one. You'll talk to pilots directly.
Strong opinions, loosely held. We argue, we ship, we update. You'll be expected to push back when we're wrong.
Hardware is real. Open Arm, R1 Light, Flexiv's, Thors. Not just sim.




What we offer

Competitive salary + meaningful equity (early-stage)
Working on robotics foundation models with a team that ships
Direct line to founders and customers
Copenhagen office, Stockholm satellite opening end of summer
Real budget for compute, hardware, and other things that matter
The usual good stuff




Apply here and or email fabian@qualiastudios.dev with whatever best represents your work and/or a resume.

We're a small, technical team in Copenhagen and Stockholm building this for manufacturers, in partnership with top companies. Qualia has raised roughly USD 10M to pursue the hardest problems in robotics.


We're hiring a research engineer to push the model side of our research. VLAs, WAMs, that work on real customer data. RL that closes the gap on precision tasks. World models, UMI, EgoData, that let us iterate faster than collecting another 1,000 episodes of teleop.


This is not a research role at a lab. This is a research engineer role at a startup. Your work ships to customers, runs on robots, and gets judged by whether the task succeeds.


What you'll work on

  • VLA fine-tuning at the edge of what's possible
  • VLAs on customer and internal data. Make them work where the base model fails.
  • Build the recipe. Subtask decomposition, stage classifiers, relative actions, action chunking, normalization. Everything that turns "we got 30% success" into "we got 99% success."
  • Push the data pipeline upstream so that fine-tuning is repeatable across robots and tasks, not a one-off.


RL on top of VLAs

  • Residual policies for high-precision sub-movements (key insertion, peg-in-hole, force-sensitive contact).
  • RL-driven training loops on top of VLAs and WAMs.
  • Sim-to-real where it accelerates a real customer task, not for its own sake.


World models and synthetic data

  • Build the workflow that turns a small amount of real teleop into a much larger training corpus.
  • Decide what's worth using in our platform and what isn't.
  • You'll own model side end to end: data, training, inference, evaluation, iteration.


Who we're looking for:

Required

  • Strong research track record OR strong applied robotics ML track record. Either path works.
  • Hands-on with at least one VLA stack. Trained one, evaluated one, debugged one. Not just read the paper.
  • Comfortable with the main ML frameworks. Comfortable on Linux. Comfortable on a real robot (or eager to be).
  • Comfortable with messy data and the realization that 80% of the work is making the data not lie to you.
  • Comfortable shipping. Models in production beat models in arxiv at this company.


Bonus

  • Real teleoperation or data collection experience
  • RL beyond toy environments
  • Experience with sim-to-real, world models, or video-conditioned generation
  • Familiarity with VLAs
  • You've shipped a robot policy that a non-engineer used and didn't break


What we don't care about

  • Whether you have a PhD or you don't
  • Whether you've worked at a famous lab or you haven't
  • Whether your background is industry, academia, or self-taught


We care that you've done the thing.


How we work

  • Small team, fast loop. Around 10 people. Repos move fast. No PR purgatory.
  • Real customers from day one. You'll talk to pilots directly.
  • Strong opinions, loosely held. We argue, we ship, we update. You'll be expected to push back when we're wrong.
  • Hardware is real. Open Arm, R1 Light, Flexiv's, Thors. Not just sim.


What we offer

  • Competitive salary + meaningful equity (early-stage)
  • Working on robotics foundation models with a team that ships
  • Direct line to founders and customers
  • Copenhagen office, Stockholm satellite opening end of summer
  • Real budget for compute, hardware, and other things that matter
  • The usual good stuff


Apply here and or email fabian@qualiastudios.dev with whatever best represents your work and/or a resume.

Title: Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer (Freelance)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Senior Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities:


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Requirements:


At least 5+ years of relevant experience in data engineering, web scraping, automation, or software development (required)
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or related technical fields is a plus
Candidates should have a strong technical foundation and practical experience with scripting, automation, and AI-assisted workflows. We are looking for specialists who can solve non-trivial problems, work confidently with LLMs, and systematically collect, structure, and validate data from diverse sources. A methodical, detail-oriented approach and the ability to work independently are essential
Strong experience in Python web scraping (BeautifulSoup, Selenium or similar), including dynamic content (JS, AJAX, infinite scroll) and APIs via proxies
Proven ability to extract data from complex structures (hierarchies, archived pages, inconsistent HTML)
Solid background in data cleaning, normalization, and validation, delivering structured datasets (CSV, JSON, Google Sheets)
Demonstrated experience handling anti-bot mechanisms and dynamic site structures at scale
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or equivalent) and containerization (Docker) as part of real workflows
Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, OpenRouter, or similar) applied to automation tasks
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
A link to GitHub is a plus
English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)


Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $45 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Research Scientist
Place: Birkerød, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us to Make an Impact

Innovation is in our DNA. We invented supercontinuum lasers and photonic crystal fibers and we constantly reimagine how our products can be used and improved. Our customers use our products in medical and life science research, quantum computing and space exploration.

We seek curious, creative and collaborative people to work in our cutting-edge company. Can you see yourself working in teams and providing solutions for innovators?

If you wish to apply, please contact us with your CV / Résumé. If you have a cover letter, please save this in one document with your CV / Résumé as you are only able to upload one document.

We look forward to hearing from you!

THE ROLE

You will be joining our highly skilled Future Technologies, Optical Engineering team to develop solutions for new laser systems and research projects. You will be working on various laser and optoelectronics technologies, utilising elements of NKTP's existing products and evaluating new technologies to incorporate into NKTP's technology roadmap. Main responsibilities may include but not limited to General Product/Process Research Science and is focused on scientific research, development, and testing of new products, processes, and raw materials .The daily work may be in close collaboration with the other departments across optical, electronic, and mechanical teams, as well as with external project partners from industry and academia.

Personal Skills We Seek


Be responsible for own work and self-driven in daily work
Enjoy working closely with experts in optics, electronics, and mechanical for design and problem solving
Able to take ownership of project work packages ensuring timely delivery and reporting when needed


Relevant technical skills


PhD in Optics or similar preferred
Laser design theory - ideally in fiber
Experience with laser feedback and control
Laser lab experience - e.g. free-space optics alignment, testing and measurement
Optical system modelling - e.g. split-step GNLSE, rate-equation-based amplifier modelling
Familiarity with Python, Matlab, and MS Office tools
English (written and spoken)


WE OFFER

Professional challenges in a highly dynamic and growing business environment with enormous potential for further growth. An opportunity to become part of a successful, enthusiastic, development team in an informal workplace. Competitive employment terms and benefits, good work-life balance, and influence on own tasks. Experienced and skilled colleagues with highly diverse backgrounds in an international organization.

APPLY TODAY

We plan to invite candidates for interviews as soon as possible. If you think you have what it takes, please send your CV to us and we will be in contact soon.

Keep your personal data safe

NKT Photonics ("The Company") take responsibly for the protection of your personal data. Accordingly, upon receiving your job application and additional material you choose to submit, the Company keeps a safe and secure environment and ensures confidentiality.

You also have a role to play in keeping your personal data safe. We request you only submit personal data about yourself in your job application, CV / Résumé or other relevant material you choose to provide to us that is relevant for the specific job opening you are applying for.

Please consider whether there is personal information you are about to submit, such as health information, information on family relations, information on trade union membership, etc. that is not necessary for our recruitment process or the job opening you are applying for. Please also be aware that certificates or other documents are not necessary for the vacancy. We urgently request you to redact/cross out such information before you submit your job application.

Thank you.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed.




Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden to understand and optimise their taxes. We automate tax tracking, surface deductions people didn't know they had, and give users the insight they need to make better financial decisions — all connected directly to the Danish tax authority.




We're an independent company of around 50 people based in Copenhagen, with a 4.7-star rating and a simple mission: make financial insight accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford an accountant.




We have an ambitious multi-year vision for where we take the product next. This role owns the ML systems that make it possible.







About the role




Skatteguiden sits on a rich proprietary dataset — years of tax and financial data across more than a million Danes. That data fuels the intelligence behind our product, and the ML systems built on top of it are what turn it into something users feel.




You'll architect and orchestrate those ML systems — how we run experiments, compare models, deploy them, and manage them cleanly in production. The systems themselves serve user-facing insights: think surfacing opportunities users would otherwise miss, and helping them understand the consequences of financial decisions before they make them.




We have models in production today, but the deployment, serving, and lifecycle architecture deserves a dedicated owner who's done this before.




You'll join the AI Products team as the senior ML profile, working closely with our existing Data Scientist, our AI Engineer, and our incoming Applied AI Engineer. The team is small and dynamic, and this role carries meaningful technical leadership for the ML systems across Skatteguiden.







What you'll do




- Architect and orchestrate our ML layer. Design how we run experiments, compare models, deploy them, and keep them healthy in production. Models, features, pipelines, and the platform that connects them.

- Be the data source expert for the AI team. Partner with the data and backend teams, get the right data in the right shape for ML, and make that pipeline reliable.

- Build systems that compound. Personalisation and pattern detection that become more useful as more users are onboarded.

- Own your systems in production. Training, evaluation, rollout, monitoring, retraining. End-to-end.

- Set the technical bar. As the senior ML profile, you help shape how the team thinks about modelling, evaluation, and production ML discipline.







Who we're looking for




You're a senior ML engineer who has shipped production ML at scale and wants to build something with genuine user impact. You care about models, but you care more about whether they ship, whether they work, and whether they actually change what a user sees.




If you're excited about the task of lifting MLOps to the next level — enabling both rapid experimentation and reliable deployment of our models and agentic systems — we'd like to hear from you.




We also want to be upfront: priorities will shift, and the first months will include some classic data engineering work alongside the ML systems work. If LLMs are the only thing you want to do, this isn't the role.







Must-have




- Senior hands-on experience shipping production ML systems. You've owned models end-to-end: features, training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, iteration. We want someone who can sit across from us and say "I've seen this, this is how it scales."

- Strong MLOps. Backend serving patterns, model-serving optimisation, deployment architecture, lifecycle management. This is the gap we're filling.

- Experience or strong interest in agentic systems and LLM-specific deployment concerns.

- Data engineering chops. Comfortable designing data pipelines and infrastructure, and partnering with the data team.

- Strong skills in Python and modern ML tooling.

- Clear communication, particularly on architectural trade-offs. You can explain to engineers, product people, and leadership why a system is designed the way it is.

- Security, privacy, and compliance awareness. You work with GDPR-sensitive data thoughtfully and you understand what ISO 27001 implies for model development and deployment.

- Genuine enthusiasm for AI. You use AI tools daily in your own work and you're always looking for new ways to apply them.







Nice-to-have




- Experience with financial, tax, or PSD2 banking data.

- Experience evaluating the LLM vs. classical ML boundary in production.

- Familiarity with Azure.

- Danish language — useful but not required. The team works in English.







Why join us




- A moat to build. Our ML layer is the technical foundation of Skatteguiden's long-term advantage. You'll be architecting and owning it.

- Real influence. Small team, high autonomy, and the decisions you make will shape our AI direction for years.

- The product matters. Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden. Your work turns their collective data into something each one of them benefits from.

- A serious roadmap. Concrete features, a multi-year vision, and an executive team that backs it.

- Solid company. Independent, profitable, growing. No VC pressure, no bloated roadmap. Just a clear mission and a team that cares.

- A company that's serious about AI. We use AI across the business, and we're only going deeper. Being a small, focused team means we can move fast on this — no lengthy approval chains, no slow rollouts.

- Good people. We take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. The culture is professional, informal, and genuinely collaborative.







Practical details




Location: Copenhagen. We're an office-first company, and it's a deliberate choice. We're moving fast, we have big ambitions, and we genuinely believe that being physically together is one of the things that makes that possible. Things move quicker when you can turn your chair and talk to someone.




We're a five-minute walk from Rådhuspladsen, stocked with everything from fruit to fizzy drinks to afternoon snacks, and lunch is at Claus Meyer's canteen just across the street — proper food, not an afterthought. If you're putting in a long day, dinner is covered too.




Start: As soon as possible.

Salary: Competitive and based on your experience.







Our lean hiring process




1. Screening call with our recruiter (30 min) ONLINE

2. Technical interview with the Head of AI Products and an AI engineer (120 min) ONSITE

3. Case / technical deep-dive with the AI Products team (60 min) ONSITE

4. Final conversation with the CTO and CEO (30 min) ONSITE

Title: Concrete Scientist
Description:
Concrete Scientist – Biomason | Denmark




Biomason is redefining how the world builds. Operating at the intersection of biotechnology and construction materials, the company has developed a breakthrough alternative to traditional cement using naturally occurring microorganisms. With a fully operational production facility in Denmark and commercial products already deployed across Europe and the US, Biomason is one of the few low carbon cement solutions proven at scale and ready for rapid expansion.

As the business scales its biocement and bioconcrete technologies into repeatable industrial production, it is strengthening its applied materials capability on site.




The Role




A highly practical, hands-on position focused on the development and optimisation of concrete and bioconcrete formulations within a live production environment.




Working closely with R&D and plant operations, you will:

Develop and test concrete mix designs, including aggregate blend optimisation
Conduct materials testing aligned to EN standards and support structured experimentation (DOE, data analysis)
Translate laboratory work into pilot and production scale applications
Work directly within plant environments, supporting mixing, pressing, and curing processes
Contribute to improving performance, durability, and consistency of products




Candidate Profile




We are seeking a Concrete Scientist / Engineer with strong applied experience across cementitious materials:

Experience in concrete, cementitious materials, or related environments
Hands-on experience with mix design and aggregate blending
Exposure to precast concrete manufacturing or similar production environments (highly desirable)
Familiarity with EN standards and materials testing methodologies
Experience within plant, production, or quality environments, with involvement in mix / recipe development
Comfortable operating across laboratory and industrial settings




You bring a practical, solution-oriented mindset, with the ability to work closely with materials, processes, and production teams to deliver real-world outcomes.




About Beaumont Bailey




Beaumont Bailey is an executive search firm specialising in industrial manufacturing, building materials, and decarbonisation-led technologies. We partner with innovative organisations to appoint technical and operational talent critical to scaling next-generation industrial solutions.

We are supporting Biomason on this appointment as part of its continued European expansion.

Beaumont Bailey is an executive search firm specialising in industrial manufacturing, building materials, and decarbonisation-led technologies. We partner with innovative organisations to appoint technical and operational talent critical to scaling next-generation industrial solutions.

We are supporting Biomason on this appointment as part of its continued European expansion.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer, Speech Recognition (ASR)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Overview

We are on a mission to ensure everyone has access to medical expertise, no matter where they are.

Half the world still lacks access to quality healthcare. Even in advanced systems, outcomes are uneven, and clinicians are overwhelmed. Medical knowledge grows faster than human capacity can keep up.

Corti exists to close that gap. Our AI platform expands access to medical expertise, reducing errors, restoring time to clinicians, and making care more affordable, accessible, and human again.

There is no quality healthcare without a quality dialogue, and no reliable AI without a strong foundation. Help us build both.

Why Corti?

Corti is the frontier lab for clinical-grade AI. We build world-leading models and infrastructure that power healthcare's most ambitious software - from EHR vendors and virtual care platforms to the health systems that care depends on. Today, Corti serves over 100 million patients annually, including the NHS.

Our flagship model, Symphony, is trained and validated on real clinical data from real patient interactions, and powers the clinical and administrative work that healthcare runs on.

We power the builders who are redefining how healthcare works, from startups creating new patient experiences to enterprises modernizing the systems that care depends on.

If you believe that AI purpose-built for medicine will define the next century of healthcare, you belong at Corti.

The Role

As a Senior Machine Learning Engineer focused on speech recognition, you will build and operate Corti’s medical-grade ASR systems. You will work on training and fine-tuning speech models at scale, building strong validation practices, and deploying low-latency inference services that are dependable in real-world conditions.

This role includes ownership across the ASR lifecycle, from data strategy and training to evaluation, serving, and monitoring. The team values careful measurement, practical engineering, and collaboration with product and platform partners.

What You’ll Be Doing


Train and fine- tune ASR models at scale, including dataset strategy, augmentation, and domain adaptation to real-world clinical audio.
Build and improve validation and evaluation frameworks, including WER and targeted analysis across speakers, environments, devices, and clinical terminology.
Deploy and operate ASR inference services with focus on reliability, latency, and efficiency in production.
Optimize inference latency and throughput, including batching strategies, model export choices, and hardware-aware profiling.
Build and maintain APIs and services in frameworks like FastAPI, Kafka, and NVIDIA Triton, and deploy and run them on Kubernetes.
Take technical ownership of core ASR components, shaping best practices for modelling, evaluation, and production reliability across the team supporting the growth of engineers working on speech systems.
Work closely with product and platform teams on safe rollouts, monitoring, and continuous improvement based on real-world feedback.


Technologies you may work with


PyTorch
Apache Kafka
NVIDIA Triton Inference Server
FastAPI for ML APIs and services
Kubernetes for deployment and operations
Common MLOps tooling for experiment tracking, model versioning, and monitoring


What You Bring


Strong programming skills in Python and the ability to contribute to production-grade codebases.
Hands-on experience in speech recognition and ASR, including at least some of the following:
Training or finetuning end-to-end ASR models (CTC/TDT/AED) at meaningful scale.
Designing evaluation and validation approaches, including WER and deeper error analysis.
Deploying and operating ASR services in production, including performance and reliability work.
Experience building ML systems that can be deployed and operated, including pipelines, CI and CD practices, and monitoring.
Clear communication and collaboration skills across research, engineering, and product.
A Master’s degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, statistics, physics, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
Nice to have: Experience with multilingual ASR, streaming inference, noisy audio conditions, or healthcare privacy and compliance constraints. Experience with Go.

Life at Corti


You will be reporting to the Director of Engineering
The position is full-time and starts as soon as possible
Hybrid working environment in our Copenhagen Office
Equipment provided by Corti


Ready to dive into the world of Corti? Hit that 'Apply' button, and let's start working together on reshaping the dialogue in healthcare, making a real difference for millions of patient outcomes around the world.

🤝 Bringing in top talent from all backgrounds is crucial in our pursuit to improve the world of healthcare. We encourage applications from all people and do not discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, and/or disability status.﻿

At Corti, experience comes in many forms, and we’re passionate about creating teams with a multitude of perspectives! If you believe your experience is close to what we’re looking for but not an exact match, we still hope you’ll consider applying!

Title: Junior Unity Developer & XR tester (Part time student job)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you interested in helping develop and test digital solutions that can make a difference and help save lives?




Come join the Strategic Research team at Laerdal Copenhagen!




THE JOB

As our new Junior Unity Developer and XR tester, you will become part of our Strategic Research team, focusing on XR Production.

The Laerdal Strategic Research (SR) team is a diverse and experienced group specializing in research, development, and international collaboration.




The SR team focuses on scaling lifesaving impact by supporting lifesavers through better training, equipment, and system. Through generating evidence and supporting evidenced solutions, the SR team directly supports Laerdal’s overarching mission of Helping Save Lives.




The SR team connects science and technology to real-world impact by maintaining close, ethical collaboration with global research partners and key opinion leaders through collaborative research and meaningful innovation that advance lifesaving solutions.




The SR team works in an iterative, exploratory way - building, testing, learning, and refining ideas together with internal and external partners.




In this position, you will become part of our office in Copenhagen, and work closely with colleagues, stakeholders, and partners across Laerdal. You should expect ongoing collaboration with the SR team in Stavanger, Norway, as well as some travel. This may include:

Demos and prototype evaluations
Partner and stakeholder meetings
Conferences and events
Occasional visits to Laerdal’s headquarter in Stavanger







Your day-to-day responsibilities will be:




Prototype Development

Assist in the design and development of prototypes using Unity under the supervision of senior team members.
Help set up XR scenarios, implement basic functionality, and prepare prototype builds.
Provide feedback and suggestions to improve prototypes and user experience.




Testing & Quality Assurance

Execute manual test procedures thoroughly and accurately report, track, and verify defects.
Reproduce, investigate, and re-test incidents reported by customers or partners.
Contribute to usability, accessibility, performance, and security evaluation of prototypes.




Hardware, Headsets & Build Management

Set up and maintain XR headsets and related hardware.
Upload, manage, and maintain prototype builds and management software.
Ensure devices and tools are updated and functioning correctly.




Collaboration & Processes

Participate in meetings and research processes.
Perform or request code/design reviews and seek guidance when needed.
Assist with tasks outside primary discipline when relevant.
Update documentation, confluence pages, and user manuals.
Communicate challenges proactively with team and stakeholders.




You are...

Passionate and curious about new technology.
Currently pursuing studies in Computer Science or a closely related discipline.
Familiar with testing approaches and concepts.
Proficient with C# or other object-oriented programming languages
Knowledgeable of game engines such as Unity
Detail oriented and structured
Proactive, self-motivated and have a can-do attitude.
You are motivated by Laerdal’s mission of helping save lives.




It would be desirable if you:

are familiar with Git (or other version control systems)
have basic understanding of UI and UX principles.
XR knowledge is a plus but not required.
3D software knowledge is a plus but not required




Your Availability

Please, note that your application will not be considered unless:

You have at least 3 semesters left on your longest planned education.
You can work at least 12 hours a week.




Interested?

Apply with your CV and a short motivation explaining why this role speaks to you. (Remember, LinkedIn allows only one document, so please merge your CV and motivation letter into one file.)




If you have any questions that might keep you from applying, please reach out by e-mail to recruiting manager Bo Søhøj on bo.sohoj@laerdal.com




We’ll review applications and set up interviews on an ongoing basis.




THE COMPANY

Laerdal Copenhagen is the center of excellence for Laerdal's digital learning solutions. We provide best-in-class digital learning products, helping doctors and nurses around the world save lives.

With a unique mix of talents - ranging from medical doctors and user-experience designers to software engineers and talents from the video game industry - we work with international partners to deliver cutting-edge digital learning solutions that help save lives. We offer a collaborative and dynamic workplace characterized by a can-do attitude, an appreciation of diversity, flexible working conditions, and a casual atmosphere in an old warehouse in the center of Copenhagen.

You can get a feel of who we are in this video: https://vimeo.com/917084721/73a5d9e58a

Title: Graduate Program Data Science & Analytics - Denmark
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?


Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.


Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:


You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
You are fluent in English and Danish.
You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
You have the legal right to work in Denmark (e.g., residency, visa).
You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

Apply Now!

Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity. Apply today and discover what's in it for you!

Please ensure both your CV and all answers in the application form are completed in English.

About Us

Are you interested in joining one of Europe's leading PayTech companies? Do you want to be part of a diverse team, collaborating with colleagues from across Europe? If so, you're in the right place!

About The Team

We are the result of merging Nexi, Nets, and Sia, with a presence in over 25 countries. With over 80 years of experience, we offer plenty of opportunities for personal and professional growth

At Nexi, we learn, adapt, and think like entrepreneurs. Discover more about our culture by visiting this page: https://www.nexigroup.com/en/people/life-at-nexi/overview/?utm_source=career-platform&utm_medium=job-openings&utm_content=about-the-team

Title: R&D researcher intern
Description:
Overview

Keysight is at the forefront of technology innovation, delivering breakthroughs and trusted insights in electronic design, simulation, prototyping, test, manufacturing, and optimization. Our ~16,800 employees create world-class solutions in communications, 5G, automotive, energy, quantum, aerospace, defense, and semiconductor markets for customers in over 100 countries. Learn more about what we do.

Our award-winning culture embraces a bold vision of where technology can take us and a passion for tackling challenging problems with industry-first solutions. We believe that when people feel a sense of belonging, they can be more creative, innovative, and thrive at all points in their careers.

Do you want to work with Software development on the cutting edge of Research and Development of telecoms? Then consider joining Keysight Technologies as a Student worker in Aalborg! We are taking world-leading Keysight tools and methodologies and using them to push our understanding of how cellular networks will evolve beyond 5G and towards 6G.

As a student in Telecom, Software, or Electronics engineering you will be involved with the transformation of ideas in the field of testing and measurement into proofs of concept in concert with the small and dynamic team we have setup at Novi, conveniently near Aalborg University.

Responsibilities

If you have experience with programming and software and an interest in telecoms and a desire to see how the field is set to evolve in the next 10 years, then this position is for you!

Your work will be of a varying scope and complexity, exposing you to the day-to-day work of Research Engineers running a research lab. One day you might be working on test execution code to stress test one of our partners’ networks, the next you might be writing code that enhances the post processing of results of potential tests, and the next you might be working on creating datasets that allow us to perform in depth analysis. Your work will always be evolving as you integrate into existing teams and projects where you will collaborate and learn from experienced engineers. The work can also easily be transitioned into your University Internship and/or a Master’s thesis, as we intend to work with you until you graduate.

No matter what work you end up doing, your hours with us are flexible with regards to your university schedule – we understand what studying is like and know that there will be periods where you are more available contrasted with ones where you need to focus on studies and exams.

Qualifications


A Master’s student or a final-year Bachelor’s student
Fluent in English, both spoken and written
Interested in Software and ideally Telecoms engineering
Experienced in C++ and Python
Comfortable working in group-based, collaborative environments
Currently studying at Aalborg University (there is no support for relocation)


Careers Privacy Statement***Keysight is an Equal Opportunity Employer.***

Title: PLC Programmer
Description:
Konsulenthuset ballisager rekrutterer på vegne af Condair A/S.




Struktureret PLC Developer til global virksomhed med lokale rødder




Vil du være med, når vi udvikler og producerer vores avancerede systemer til nogle af landets største virksomheder? Motiveres du af at være i et miljø, hvor du tester og dokumenterer den software, der gør vores løsninger driftssikre og eftertragtede? Så er det måske dig, vi søger.




Hos Condair A/S leder vi lige nu efter dig, der har erfaring med udvikling, vedligeholdelse og optimering af PLC-styrede procesanlæg. Sammen med kolleger med forskellige fagligheder udvikler du de bedste løsninger for vores kunder.




Du bliver en vigtig del af et mindre, specialiseret team i Ry, hvor vores befugtningssystem, som du kommer til at arbejde med, produceres. Din rolle bliver en kombination af softwareudvikling, dokumentation og teknisk problemløsning.




Du hviler i din faglighed og erfaring

En stor del af din rolle handler om at skabe struktur og kvalitet i softwareplatformen og sikre, at løsningerne er veldokumenterede og driftssikre. Du bliver samtidig involveret i udviklingen af vores nye softwareplatform og får mulighed for at præge måden, vi arbejder med software og dokumentation fremadrettet.




Vi leder ikke efter en ren softwareudvikler, men en profil der har arbejdet hands-on med industrielle procesanlæg og forstår samspillet mellem software, hardware og den fysiske proces. Du har erfaring med commissioning, fejlfinding og at få anlæg i drift.




Lidt mere konkret bliver dine opgaver:

PLC-programmering
Dokumentation og beskrivelse af software
Fejlfinding og teknisk support på software og procesanlæg
Test af software på vores egne anlæg og maskiner
Samarbejde med el-teknikere, produktion og øvrige tekniske kolleger
Sparring med servicefolk, salg og kunder ved tekniske udfordringer
Deltagelse i videreudvikling og standardisering af softwareplatform




Det meste support løses remote, men der kan forekomme enkelte rejsedage hos kunder i Danmark og udlandet. At du kan skrive og tale både dansk og engelsk, er derfor en forudsætning for at kunne begå dig i rollen.




Vi opererer globalt, og vi står solidt i vores jyske værdier

Som en del af Condair vil du opleve en hverdag med høj faglig stolthed og stærke relationer mellem kollegerne. Vi hjælper hinanden, handler på vores beslutninger og går ikke på kompromis med kvaliteten.




Vores løsninger findes på de fleste kontinenter, og vi er med, når vores kunder åbner nye afdelinger rundt om i verden. I al jysk beskedenhed er vi ret stolte af at kunne kalde os lokale med vores produktion i Ry, samtidig med at vores løsninger krydser landegrænser.




Hvis du kan se dig selv i en rolle, hvor ansvar og indflydelse bliver givet dér, hvor det bliver taget, så kunne vi godt være et match for hinanden. Hos os får du mulighed for at arbejde med nicheteknologi i både danske og internationale projekter. Som virksomhed ved vi, at vi også skal sælge os selv, og vi kan da godt nævne alle personalegoderne (såsom sommerfest, julefrokost, massageordning, sundhedsforsikring, betalt internet, personaleforening, frugtordning mm.). Men vi tror på, at det er vores kollegers høje anciennitet (+10 år), der siger det bedst.




Nok om os, mere om dig

Din erfaring med PLC/automation inden for industrien er afgørende i denne rolle. Samtidig er det din gode forståelse for procesanlæg, der gør, at du lykkes med at give vores kunder det, de efterspørger. Du kan eksempelvis være automatiktekniker, maskinmester, elektriker med stærk PLC-erfaring eller ingeniør.




Det vigtigste er ikke din uddannelsesmæssige baggrund, men at du har hands-on erfaring med industrielle procesanlæg og komponenter som pumper, ventiler, sensorer, tanke og frekvensomformere. Du har forståelse for flow-, tryk- og niveaustyring og er vant til at arbejde med software, der styrer reelle processer og produkter.




Du forstår værdien af veldokumenteret software og arbejder naturligt struktureret med både programmering og dokumentation. Samtidig har du teknisk pondus, tager ansvar og trives i samarbejdet med både kolleger og kunder.




Vi har ikke automatiseret ansøgningsprocessen, men...

Vi har gjort det nemt for dig at søge. Vi bruger ikke ansøgninger, men vi vil meget gerne have et motiveret CV fra dig.




Du kan læse mere om, hvad det indebærer her.

Vi lukker stillingsopslaget, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat. Vi opfordrer dig derfor til at søge hurtigst muligt.




Vi får hjælp af Konsulenthuset ballisager til at håndtere rekrutteringsprocessen. Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte seniorkonsulent Peter Nibe Hansen på 8175 6603 eller pnh@ballisager.com.




Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.







Om Condair

Med mere end 40 års erfaring i Danmark er vi stolte af at have et stærkt lokalt team. Vi er omkring 60 engagerede medarbejdere fordelt på kontorer i både Ry og Slagelse og et hold af højtspecialiserede serviceteknikere, der dækker hele landet.




Siden 2013 har vi været en del af den schweiziske Condair-koncern, hvor vi nu er mere end 1.000 medarbejdere på verdensplan, hvilket giver os adgang til global viden og ressourcer. Denne kombination gør det muligt for vores medarbejdere at vokse i et internationalt miljø, mens de stadig kan bevare den lokale forankring.




Hos Condair ved vi, at vores medarbejdere er vores vigtigste ressource. Derfor er der plads til faglig udvikling, kreativitet og balance mellem arbejde og fritid. Vi har en åben og inkluderende kultur, der gør, at vi kan løfte i flok. Vi værdsætter samarbejde og fælles indsats, og hos os skaber vi resultater ved at spille hinanden stærkere som et samlet hold.

Show more

Konsulenthuset ballisager rekrutterer på vegne af Condair A/S.


Struktureret PLC Developer til global virksomhed med lokale rødder


Vil du være med, når vi udvikler og producerer vores avancerede systemer til nogle af landets største virksomheder? Motiveres du af at være i et miljø, hvor du tester og dokumenterer den software, der gør vores løsninger driftssikre og eftertragtede? Så er det måske dig, vi søger.


Hos Condair A/S leder vi lige nu efter dig, der har erfaring med udvikling, vedligeholdelse og optimering af PLC-styrede procesanlæg. Sammen med kolleger med forskellige fagligheder udvikler du de bedste løsninger for vores kunder.


Du bliver en vigtig del af et mindre, specialiseret team i Ry, hvor vores befugtningssystem, som du kommer til at arbejde med, produceres. Din rolle bliver en kombination af softwareudvikling, dokumentation og teknisk problemløsning.


Du hviler i din faglighed og erfaring

En stor del af din rolle handler om at skabe struktur og kvalitet i softwareplatformen og sikre, at løsningerne er veldokumenterede og driftssikre. Du bliver samtidig involveret i udviklingen af vores nye softwareplatform og får mulighed for at præge måden, vi arbejder med software og dokumentation fremadrettet.


Vi leder ikke efter en ren softwareudvikler, men en profil der har arbejdet hands-on med industrielle procesanlæg og forstår samspillet mellem software, hardware og den fysiske proces. Du har erfaring med commissioning, fejlfinding og at få anlæg i drift.


Lidt mere konkret bliver dine opgaver:

  • PLC-programmering
  • Dokumentation og beskrivelse af software
  • Fejlfinding og teknisk support på software og procesanlæg
  • Test af software på vores egne anlæg og maskiner
  • Samarbejde med el-teknikere, produktion og øvrige tekniske kolleger
  • Sparring med servicefolk, salg og kunder ved tekniske udfordringer
  • Deltagelse i videreudvikling og standardisering af softwareplatform

Title: Forward Deployed AI Scientist, Internship, Denmark - BCG X
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.

We Are BCG X

We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.

What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.

You'll be part of our rapidly growing team who will have a chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.

Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, creative, and communicative.

We Are Looking For Dedicated Individuals With a Passion For Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research And Redefining Organizations Into AI Led Innovative Companies. Successful Candidates Possess The Following


Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
Significant experience applying advanced analytics to business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
Have strong project management skills


What You'll Bring

Requirements

Currently enrolled in a university master’s or PhD degree program in Computer Research Science, Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research, or related field

Technologies

Programming Languages: Python

#BCGXjob

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.

BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

Title: Senior MLOps Engineer / AI Platform Specialist
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Twoday is the leading digital transformation partner in Northern Europe with a global presence. With approximately 3,000 technologies, we collaborate with the most admired private and public organizations to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions. Our deep industry expertise spans Data & AI, software development, digital experiences, and business applications. Operating across the Nordics and Lithuania, our team generated a revenue of 280 million euros in 2023. We serve over 8,000 customers, supporting their digital transformation journeys.

Senior MLOps Engineer / AI Platform Specialist

About The Role

We are looking for a Senior MLOps Engineer / AI Platform Specialist to help design, build, and operate production-grade machine learning platforms.

This is a hands-on role onsite in Copenhagen where you will work at the intersection of data, infrastructure, and software engineering – ensuring that machine learning solutions are scalable, reliable, and ready for real-world use.

You will work closely with data scientists, developers, and stakeholders to bring AI solutions from experimentation into stable and secure production environments.

You will be responsible for:

MLOps & platform engineering


Design and implement end-to-end MLOps pipelines
Enable reliable deployment, versioning, and lifecycle management of ML models
Establish best practices for model governance, reproducibility, and traceability


Cloud & infrastructure


Build and maintain ML platforms in cloud environments (Azure, GCP or similar)
Design hybrid architectures combining cloud and on-premise solutions where needed
Implement Infrastructure as Code and automated provisioning


CI/CD & automation


Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for ML and data workflows
Automate model training, testing, and deployment processes
Ensure robust and scalable delivery pipelines


Monitoring & reliability


Implement monitoring for models and data pipelines (performance, drift, availability)
Ensure stable and secure operation of ML solutions in production
Drive continuous improvements based on performance and usage


We Are Looking For Someone Who


Has experience with MLOps and running ML models in production
Has worked with cloud platforms such as Azure Machine Learning, GCP (Vertex AI), or similar
Has strong programming skills (e.g. Python)
Has experience with containerisation and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes)
Has experience with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure automation
Fluent spoken and written Danish is a requirement


It is a plus if you:


Have worked with modern ML frameworks (e.g. PyTorch, MLflow or similar)
Have experience with LLMs or advanced AI architectures
Have worked in regulated or compliance-driven environments
Are able to communicate complex technical topics clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders


About You


You take ownership and are comfortable working in complex environments
You are pragmatic and focus on delivering real-world value
You enjoy working across disciplines and bridging gaps between teams
You are motivated by building solutions that work in production


What We Offer

Twoday offers a flexible and collaborative working environment with strong professional communities across the Nordics.

We offer:


The opportunity to work with AI solutions in production – not just proof of concepts
A central role in shaping modern AI and data platforms
Opportunities for learning, certification, and professional development
A strong technical environment with knowledge sharing and collaboration
A comprehensive employee package including pension, health insurance, and additional benefits


The employment

You will be permanently employed by Twoday and work as a consultant with a public sector client. Twoday will be your employer, offering a strong professional community, skills development, and clear career paths — while you contribute to an important and meaningful project with the client.

The process

Introductory call with a Talent Acquisition Partner

Online interview with the Hiring Manager

Technical interview

On-site interview in Nordhavn

Diversity & inclusion

Do you not meet all the requirements? Studies show that women and minorities are less likely to apply if they don’t meet every qualification. At twoday, we are committed to building an inclusive workplace where everyone is welcome.

If this role excites you, we encourage you to apply.

Title: Signal Processing Developer – New Sports (C++/Python)
Description:
Job Description

Join Trackman’s New Sports team as a Signal Processing Developer and build cutting-edge radar and vision algorithms for American football. Turn advanced signal processing into real-time, production-ready solutions that power elite sports performance.

Trackman Is Looking For a Signal Processing Developer To Join Our New Sports Team At Our Aarhus Office. As a Member Of Our Team Within The Tracking Department, You'll Work With Cutting-edge Radar And Computer Vision-based Tracking Technologies, Delivering Accurate Tracking Data Through Tasks Such As


Design and implement signal processing and tracking algorithms
Lead new feature design on American football
Fuse radar signals with video and other sensors
Create offline evaluation tools, datasets, and accuracy metrics
Optimize for real-time performance and robustness
Integrate algorithms into modular, testable components
Iterate based on on-field feedback and experiment


Trackman is specialized in creating state-of-the-art radar and vision solutions that are the gold standard in sports like golf, baseball, American football, and soccer. This role is for scientists and engineers who are passionate about turning complex physical models into high-performance, production-ready code.

What do we offer?


Highly skilled colleagues and a collaborative culture
Real product impact in elite sports training
An opportunity to work with one of the most advanced and robust tracking systems in the world.
International environment with 35+ nationalities
Social activities and a vibrant engineering community


Job Requirements


MSc or PhD in electrical engineering, computer science, applied math or similar
Strong signal processing skills
Proficient in C++ and Python for production code
Several years of relevant industry or research experience
Comfortable with Linux and Git


Our ideal candidates for this position possess significant experience in leading project development and classical signal processing.

What could also be relevant?


Project experience from the audio of medical industry
Real-time distributed design patterns and code reuse
Machine learning and neural networks
Mathematical modeling
Software testing frameworks
Interest in American football


Join the home of a powerful sports brand and a one-of-a-kind technology

Our proprietary technology is based on expert knowledge about radar, computer vision, data, and software engineering. Our solutions are developed by specialists who endlessly explore and challenge new technical boundaries. Trackman’s blend of cutting-edge technology, sports, and continued growth make our company an outstanding place to work.

Our work culture is entrepreneurial, ambitious, and rewarding as you get to collaborate with inspiring colleagues and interact with the leaders of golf, baseball, and football. Based on a unique brand reputation, we are proud to work in close partnership with the top athletes, organizations, and teams that rely on our technology to stay ahead of the race.

We are a Danish company founded, owned, and managed since 2003 by three entrepreneurs with a strong passion for sports. Today, we have a global footprint with our headquarters located in Denmark (Hørsholm) and offices in New York, Phoenix, Seoul and Tokyo. We employ over 1000+ people worldwide, half of which work in Denmark, where most hardware and software products are developed, tested, and manufactured.

Where innovation happens

At Trackman, we know that great people make great products. We believe that teams thrive, and innovation sparks in an atmosphere where people are encouraged to think for themselves, where everybody’s voices are heard, and where the best ideas prevail in the pursuit of exceptional solutions. This mix makes Trackman an inspiring company – for our customers and colleagues.

Share our Passion!

For additional information about the position, please contact Troels Bastholm at troels@trackman.recruitee.com Applications are continuously assessed, so please send your application as soon as possible.
