Title: Senior Process Chemist
Description:
About Vateris

Vateris is a climate-tech company developing carbon-to-materials technologies for the construction industry. Our technology permanently mineralises captured CO₂ into high-performance materials that enable major cement reduction while maintaining or improving concrete performance.

Backed by investors including Holcim and GOLDBECK, Vateris is now preparing for the next stage of industrial scale-up.

We are planning delivery of a ~£15 million demonstration plant from Q4 2026 onwards and are looking for exceptional technical talent with highly relevant industrial backgrounds to help scale the technology toward commercial deployment.




The Role

We are seeking a highly capable Senior Process Chemist to lead optimisation and industrialisation of our carbon mineralisation process.

This is not a purely academic research role. We are specifically looking for candidates with strong industrial experience in scalable chemical processes and real-world manufacturing environments.

The successful candidate will work closely with engineering, operations and scale-up teams as we transition toward demonstration and commercial plant deployment.

Responsibilities

Lead optimisation of CO₂ mineralisation and precipitation processes
Develop robust, scalable and reproducible chemical operations
Support pilot plant operation and future demonstration plant scale-up
Investigate reaction kinetics, impurities, process variability and operational stability
Work closely with process engineers and plant operations teams
Develop SOPs, operating envelopes and process control strategies
Analyse plant and laboratory data to improve performance and reliability
Support techno-economic optimisation and industrial feedstock integration
Contribute to patent generation and technical documentation




Requirements

PhD in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Materials Science or a related discipline from a top 5 UK university or equivalent leading European institution, or equivalent industrial experience in a highly relevant field
Typically 5+ years of industrial experience in process chemistry, chemical manufacturing, pilot plants or industrial scale-up environments
Strong understanding of industrial/process chemistry and scalable chemical operations
Experience working with precipitation, crystallisation, slurry systems, mineral processing or related process chemistries
Practical problem-solving mindset with the ability to troubleshoot technical and operational challenges
Comfortable working in pilot plant, demonstration plant or industrial manufacturing settings
Ability to work effectively in a fast-moving and collaborative scale-up environment
Strong communication and teamwork skills across multidisciplinary technical teams




Highly Desirable

Experience from industries such as specialty chemicals, industrial minerals, fertilisers, carbon capture, hydrometallurgy or other process industries
Experience supporting scale-up, commissioning or optimisation of industrial chemical processes
Experience in startups, scale-up companies or fast-growth industrial environments
Ability to translate laboratory development into robust industrial operation

Title: Regional Lead
Description:
Job Title: Regional Lead (x7)

Function: Modernisation Programme - Business Integration

Location: Trust Based – with the expectation to travel to Nottingham as required (more information below)

Contract type: Two Year Internal Secondment

Salary: The annual salary for this post is negotiable depending on experience – please feel free to get in touch for further discussion

Closing Date: Sunday, 14 June 2026

NHS Supply Chain currently has an opportunity for seven Regional Leads to join our team during an exciting period of transformation, working to make it easier for the NHS to put patients first. We are looking for Regional Leads to cover the following locations:


1 x Midlands
1 x South West
1 x South East
1 x North West
1 x North East & Yorkshire
1 x London
1 x East of England


The Supply Chain Modernisation Regional Lead acts as the primary link between the programme and NHS Trusts, ensuring effective engagement in solution design, implementation and adoption. The role brings frontline expertise into programme decision-making and supports Trusts to successfully embed agreed processes across Acute and Non-Acute settings. The aim is to ensure that Customer insights and processes are considered at every stage of this programme.

Every day you will …


Enable effective engagement, acting as the main conduit between the programme and NHS Trusts to ensure alignment, clarity and shared ownership.
Shape solution design by participating in programme workshops and providing structured feedback and recommendations.
Prepare Trusts for change by planning and supporting workshops, gathering insight, identifying process variation and consolidating regional feedback.
Support end-to-end process, working with Value Stream Leads and Process Owners on as-is documentation and to-be process design.
Drive implementation and adoption, supporting Trusts to embed agreed processes and ensure consistent application and compliance.


What can we offer you?

We Want To Reward You For Your Passion, Enthusiasm, And Hard Work So We Offer Much More Than a Competitive Salary


Hybrid working opportunities, giving you the flexibility to work collaboratively in the office and remotely.
We recognise our employees' hard work and contributions with annual bonus schemes, long service, and colleague recognition awards.
27 days holiday plus bank holidays
We are dedicated to your development, through in-house training, support, and access to external qualifications to maximise your potential.
A focus on your well-being offering 1 day of paid well-being leave and free access to the 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme
Generous pension scheme (with us contributing 12% when you contribute 6%)
Access to our Flexible Benefits Scheme, where you can choose from a variety of benefits such as Life Insurance, Critical Illness Cover, Income Protection, Health Cash Plan, Dental Insurance, and additional pension contributions that suit you.
2 days of paid volunteering leave allowing you to give back to your community.
Access to many discounts from the Blue Light Card to NHS Discounts.


NHS Supply Chain, who are we?

Our role is to support the NHS to save lives and improve health. We are a part of the NHS family, and our role is to source, deliver and supply healthcare products, services and food for NHS trusts and healthcare organisations across England and Wales.

We serve every NHS Trust and operate a national network of distribution centres, managing relationships with more than a thousand suppliers and delivering more than 8,000,000 orders each year to more than 17,000 locations. Doing all of this on behalf of the NHS gives NHS staff more time to focus on their main priority of providing excellent patient care.

What skills will help you thrive in this role?


Recognised Subject Matter Expert in key supply chain and/or procurement processes.
Detailed knowledge of Acute or Non-Acute Trust environments (ideally both).
Experience contributing to process design, standardisation and implementation.
Credibility and influence with Trust leaders and operational teams.
Structured thinking, able to gather, synthesise and articulate insight and feedback.


Want to develop your career with the NHS Supply Chain? Then apply below or reach out for an informal discussion about the role in confidence by emailing us at Careers@supplychain.nhs.uk

Our Inclusive Commitment

At NHS Supply Chain, we are committed to building an inclusive environment where difference is not only valued, but celebrated, giving everyone the opportunity to thrive in their career. Developing our people is key to our success, so if this role sounds like the right next step in your career but your experience doesn’t match perfectly with the job advert, we encourage you to still apply.

Struggling to complete our application form, and require additional support? Reach out to our Talent Acquisition team at careers@supplychain.nhs.uk who will be happy to help you with alternative ways to apply.

We reserve the right to close any vacancy from further submissions when we have received sufficient applications from which to make a shortlist. Please apply without delay if you wish to be considered for this role.

SCCL is a company Registered in England and Wales, with company number 10881715, to act as the management function of the NHS Supply Chain.

Title: AI Adoption Manager
Description:
Role Purpose

Location- Ruddington, Nottinghamshire

Level - Manager/Experienced Professional

Department - Strategy

Working Pattern - Hybrid (three days a week in the head office)

Benefits - Benefits At Ideagen

Salary - To be discussed at next stage

Mazlan is Ideagen’s AI capability and one of the most exciting developments we’ve delivered to customers in recent years. As we continue to evolve Mazlan, we’re building a new, dedicated team focused on helping customers get real value from it during their free trial period.

As an AI Adoption Manager, you’ll play a key role in supporting trial customers across your region, guiding them through Mazlan and helping them integrate it into their day-to-day work. This is a customer-facing role within a newly formed team, offering the opportunity to help shape processes, build strong regional relationships, and develop valuable expertise in AI adoption.

This role offers the chance to be part of launching something new at Ideagen, build in-demand AI adoption skills, and make a meaningful impact on customer outcomes within a defined region, with clear progression as the team grows.

Responsibilities


Owning engagement for Mazlan free-trial customers within your assigned region
Managing a portfolio of trial accounts from activation through to conversion
Delivering structured onboarding sessions, demos, and regular check-ins
Understanding customer workflows and tailoring guidance to their specific needs
Proactively identifying and addressing barriers to adoption, such as confidence, time, or technical questions
Translating Mazlan’s capabilities into practical, industry-relevant use cases
Monitoring trial health and flagging at-risk accounts early
Sharing customer feedback and insights with Product, Customer Success, and Commercial teams


Skills And Experience

You don’t need to be an AI expert to succeed in this role — curiosity, strong communication skills, and a genuine interest in helping customers get value from technology are far more important. You’re comfortable working independently, enjoy building relationships, and can balance customer needs with commercial awareness.


You are highly customer-focused and enjoy enabling others to succeed with software
You communicate clearly and confidently with both end users and senior stakeholders
You learn new tools quickly and can explain them in a clear, accessible way
You understand the link between customer success, adoption, and business growth
You are comfortable working in a regional role and building long-term relationships
Experience in customer success, onboarding, training, or implementation would be an advantage

Ideagen is the invisible force behind many things we rely on every day - from keeping airplanes soaring in the sky, to ensuring the food on our tables is safe, to helping doctors and nurses care for the sick.
